description: Perform K-means clustering to group data into specified number of clusters

variations.name: Basic K-means with 5 clusters
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['recency', 'frequency', 'monetary'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_kmeans(
    n_clusters=5,
    algorithm_name='customer_value_segments',
    random_state=42
)

variations.name: K-means with optimal cluster number detection
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['website_visits', 'session_duration', 'page_views'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_kmeans(
    find_optimal=True,
    max_clusters=10,
    algorithm_name='engagement_segments',
    random_state=42
)

variations.name: K-means with custom initialization
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['sales_velocity', 'profit_margin'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_kmeans(
    n_clusters=4,
    algorithm_name='product_performance_segments',
    init='k-means++',
    n_init=10,
    random_state=99
)

variations.name: K-means with different distance metrics
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['browsing_pages', 'time_on_site', 'bounce_rate'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_kmeans(
    n_clusters=6,
    algorithm_name='visitor_behavior_segments',
    metric='manhattan',
    random_state=555
)

description: Perform DBSCAN clustering to identify dense regions and outliers

variations.name: Basic DBSCAN for outlier detection
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['navigation_path', 'click_pattern', 'session_duration'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_dbscan(
    eps=0.5,
    min_samples=10,
    algorithm_name='navigation_pattern_clusters'
)

variations.name: DBSCAN for transaction anomaly detection
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['transaction_amount', 'transaction_frequency', 'location'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_dbscan(
    eps=0.7,
    min_samples=5,
    algorithm_name='transaction_anomaly_detection'
)

variations.name: DBSCAN with different distance metrics
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['ticket_category', 'resolution_time', 'priority_level'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_dbscan(
    eps=0.3,
    min_samples=15,
    algorithm_name='support_ticket_patterns',
    metric='cosine'
)

variations.name: DBSCAN for review similarity clustering
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['review_sentiment', 'rating', 'review_length'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_dbscan(
    eps=0.4,
    min_samples=8,
    algorithm_name='review_similarity_clusters'
)

description: Perform HDBSCAN clustering for hierarchical density-based clustering

variations.name: Basic HDBSCAN for product affinity clustering
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['co_purchase_frequency', 'category_similarity', 'price_range'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_hdbscan(
    min_cluster_size=5,
    min_samples=3,
    algorithm_name='product_affinity_clusters'
)

variations.name: HDBSCAN for customer lifetime value patterns
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['ltv', 'purchase_frequency', 'tenure'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_hdbscan(
    min_cluster_size=10,
    min_samples=5,
    algorithm_name='ltv_pattern_clusters'
)

variations.name: HDBSCAN with custom cluster selection
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['session_duration', 'page_views', 'interaction_rate'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_hdbscan(
    min_cluster_size=15,
    min_samples=7,
    algorithm_name='adaptive_behavior_clusters',
    cluster_selection_epsilon=0.1
)

variations.name: HDBSCAN for support interaction clustering
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['interaction_type', 'resolution_time', 'customer_satisfaction'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_hdbscan(
    min_cluster_size=8,
    min_samples=4,
    algorithm_name='support_interaction_clusters'
)

variations.name: Agglomerative clustering with complete linkage
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['purchase_history', 'demographics', 'engagement_metrics'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_agglomerative(
    n_clusters=4,
    linkage='complete',
    algorithm_name='customer_hierarchical_segments'
)

variations.name: Agglomerative clustering with Ward linkage
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['product_features', 'category_attributes', 'performance_metrics'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_agglomerative(
    n_clusters=6,
    linkage='ward',
    algorithm_name='product_hierarchy'
)

variations.name: Agglomerative clustering with average linkage
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['feedback_text_features', 'sentiment_scores', 'category_labels'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_agglomerative(
    n_clusters=5,
    linkage='average',
    algorithm_name='feedback_themes'
)

variations.name: Agglomerative clustering with single linkage
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | get_features(columns=['channel_performance', 'audience_metrics', 'cost_efficiency'])
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_agglomerative(
    n_clusters=3,
    linkage='single',
    algorithm_name='channel_hierarchy'
)

description: Create segments based on predefined business rules

variations.name: Basic rule-based segmentation for engagement
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
rules = {
    'High Value Active': "subscription_tier == 'premium' and activity_score > 8",
    'High Value At Risk': "subscription_tier == 'premium' and activity_score < 3",
    'Growth Potential': "subscription_tier == 'basic' and activity_score > 8",
    'Dormant': "activity_score < 2"
}
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_rule_based(
    rules=rules,
    default_segment='Other',
    algorithm_name='engagement_segments'
)

variations.name: RFM rule-based segmentation
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
rules = {
    'Champions': "recency_score >= 4 and frequency_score >= 4 and monetary_score >= 4",
    'Loyal Customers': "recency_score >= 3 and frequency_score >= 3 and monetary_score >= 3",
    'At Risk': "recency_score <= 2 and frequency_score >= 3 and monetary_score >= 3",
    'New Customers': "recency_score >= 4 and frequency_score <= 2",
    'Hibernating': "recency_score <= 2 and frequency_score <= 2"
}
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_rule_based(
    rules=rules,
    default_segment='Others',
    algorithm_name='rfm_segments'
)

variations.name: Product performance rule-based segmentation
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
rules = {
    'High Performers': "turnover_ratio > 5 and profit_margin > 0.3",
    'Cash Cows': "turnover_ratio <= 5 and turnover_ratio > 2 and profit_margin > 0.3",
    'Stars': "turnover_ratio > 5 and profit_margin <= 0.3 and profit_margin > 0.15",
    'Dogs': "turnover_ratio <= 2 and profit_margin <= 0.15"
}
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_rule_based(
    rules=rules,
    default_segment='Average Performers',
    algorithm_name='product_performance_segments'
)

variations.name: Customer health score rule-based segmentation
variations.example:
seg_pipe = SegmentationPipe.from_dataframe(data)
rules = {
    'Healthy': "usage_frequency > 20 and support_tickets < 2",
    'At Risk': "usage_frequency < 10 or (usage_frequency < 20 and support_tickets > 3)",
    'Needs Attention': "usage_frequency >= 10 and usage_frequency <= 20 and support_tickets >= 2 and support_tickets <= 3"
}
seg_pipe = seg_pipe | run_rule_based(
    rules=rules,
    default_segment='Neutral',
    algorithm_name='customer_health_segments'
)

rules.Needs Attention: usage_frequency >= 10 and usage_frequency <= 20 and support_tickets >= 2 and support_tickets <= 3

description: Generate detailed summary statistics for segmentation results

variations.name: Generate summary for customer value segments
variations.example:
summary = generate_summary(algorithm='customer_value_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"Segment profiles: {summary['segment_profiles']}")
print(f"Segment sizes: {summary['segment_sizes']}")

variations.name: Generate summary for RFM segments
variations.example:
summary = generate_summary(algorithm='rfm_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"RFM segment breakdown: {summary['segment_sizes']}")
print(f"Segment characteristics: {summary['segment_profiles']}")

variations.name: Generate summary for product performance segments
variations.example:
summary = generate_summary(algorithm='product_performance_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"Product segment characteristics: {summary['segment_profiles']}")
print(f"Segment distribution: {summary['segment_percentages']}")

variations.name: Generate summary for visitor behavior segments
variations.example:
summary = generate_summary(algorithm='visitor_behavior_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"Visitor behavior profiles: {summary['segment_profiles']}")
print(f"Segment sizes: {summary['segment_sizes']}")

variations.name: Get high value active users
variations.example:
high_value_users = get_segment_data('high_value_active', 'engagement_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"Found {len(high_value_users)} high value active users")
print(high_value_users.head())

variations.name: Get champions from RFM segments
variations.example:
champions_data = get_segment_data('Champions', 'rfm_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"Champions segment size: {len(champions_data)}")
champions_data.to_csv('champions_export.csv', index=False)

variations.name: Get high performers from product segments
variations.example:
high_performers = get_segment_data('High Performers', 'product_performance_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"High performers: {len(high_performers)} products")
print(high_performers[['product_name', 'turnover_ratio', 'profit_margin']].head())

variations.name: Get at risk customers
variations.example:
at_risk_customers = get_segment_data('At Risk', 'customer_health_segments')(seg_pipe)
print(f"At risk customers: {len(at_risk_customers)}")
print(at_risk_customers[['customer_id', 'usage_frequency', 'support_tickets']].head())

description: Compare effectiveness of different segmentation algorithms

variations.name: Compare KMeans vs rule-based approaches
variations.example:
comparison = compare_algorithms()(seg_pipe)
print(f"Algorithm comparison: {comparison}")
print(f"Best algorithm: {comparison['best_algorithm']}")

variations.name: Evaluate most actionable customer groups
variations.example:
evaluation = compare_algorithms()(seg_pipe)
print(f"Algorithm evaluation: {evaluation}")
print(f"Most actionable: {evaluation['most_actionable']}")

variations.name: Analyze clustering differences
variations.example:
analysis = compare_algorithms()(seg_pipe)
print(f"Clustering differences: {analysis}")
print(f"Algorithm performance: {analysis['performance_metrics']}")

variations.name: Compare segment stability
variations.example:
stability_comparison = compare_algorithms()(seg_pipe)
print(f"Segment stability: {stability_comparison}")
print(f"Most stable: {stability_comparison['most_stable']}")
